title: Yoga Teacher Training in Rishikesh
description: Train in the world's yoga capital, where ancient Vedic tradition meets the sacred Ganges. Rishikesh offers the most authentic yoga lineage — Hatha, Ashtanga, Vinyasa — in a disciplined ashram environment with Himalayan air, mountain views, and centuries of spiritual heritage.
airport: Jolly Grant Airport (DED), Dehradun — 35 km from Rishikesh
highlights: Daily Ganga aarti ceremony at Triveni Ghat
Authentic ashram lifestyle with Vedic meals

programs.name: 100-Hour Yoga Immersion
programs.subtitle: Foundation Course · School Certificate
programs.certification: School Certificate of Completion (pathway to 200hr RYT)
included: 3 yogic vegetarian meals daily + herbal teas
Yoga mat, blocks, strap & neti pot kit
Weekend excursion (Kunjapuri temple or Ganga aarti)

curriculum.detail: Sun Salutations A & B, standing poses, backbends, inversions, balancing asanas — Hatha & Vinyasa flow fundamentals
Nadi Shodhana, Kapalabhati, Bhastrika, Ujjayi, Bhramari — breath awareness & energy regulation
Jala Neti, Trataka, Nauli Kriya — yogic cleansing practices
Mantra meditation, body scan, Yoga Nidra, concentration practices
Eight Limbs of Yoga (Patanjali), four paths of yoga, introduction to yoga history
Musculoskeletal system, breath mechanics, safe alignment principles
Basic cueing, class structure, demonstration, observation
Sanskrit alphabet, Gayatri mantra, Om chanting, Vedic mantras

faq.q: Do I need prior yoga experience for the 100hr program?
faq.a: No prior experience is required. The 100-hour program is designed as a foundation course, perfect for dedicated beginners and regular practitioners who want to deepen their practice.

faq.q: Is the 100hr Yoga Alliance certified?
faq.a: The 100-hour program issues a school certificate of completion. It is not a standalone Yoga Alliance designation, but it counts as the first module toward a full 200-hour RYT certification when you continue your training.

faq.q: What style of yoga is taught?
faq.a: We teach a multi-style curriculum covering Hatha (foundational alignment), Ashtanga (structured sequences), and Vinyasa (breath-linked flow). You graduate with a well-rounded foundation rather than being limited to a single lineage.

faq.q: What is the accommodation like?
faq.a: Students stay in clean, comfortable shared rooms at our partner yoga school near the Ganges. Rooms have attached bathrooms, hot water, WiFi, and basic amenities. All meals are sattvic (pure) vegetarian.

faq.q: Can I book 100hr and then continue to 200hr immediately?
faq.a: Yes — many students complete the 100hr and immediately enrol in the second 100hr module to earn their RYT 200. Ask us about back-to-back batch scheduling to plan a continuous 28-day stay.

programs.name: 200-Hour Yoga Teacher Training
programs.subtitle: Yoga Alliance RYT 200 Certification
programs.style: Multi-style: Hatha · Ashtanga Primary · Vinyasa Flow
included: 3 yogic vegetarian meals daily + herbal teas
Yoga kit: mat, 2 blocks, strap, blanket, neti pot
Airport pickup from Dehradun (Jolly Grant Airport)
not_included: Yoga Alliance registration fee ($120 USD — payable directly to YA)

curriculum.detail: 60+ classical asanas with alignment, contraindications, modifications and hands-on adjustment techniques
Full Primary Series with vinyasas, driste, breath count — taught progressively over 25 days
Creative sequencing, peak pose methodology, transitions, theming and pacing a flow class
10 techniques: Nadi Shodhana, Kapalabhati, Bhastrika, Ujjayi, Sheetali, Bhramari, Surya & Chandra Bheda, Viloma, Sama Vritti
Mantra meditation, Vipassana, loving-kindness, Yoga Nidra facilitation, mindfulness, Trataka
Patanjali Yoga Sutras (all 4 chapters), Bhagavad Gita (key chapters), Upanishads, Sankhya philosophy, four purusharthas
Skeletal, muscular, respiratory, nervous and endocrine systems as they apply to yoga. Safe practice principles, injury prevention
Voice, cues, assists, sequencing theory, class design, Sanskrit names, ethics of teaching, business basics
6 classical yogic cleansing practices including Jala Neti, Sutra Neti, Nauli, Trataka, Kapalbhati, Dhauti
28 mudras, 4 primary bandhas, 7-chakra system — energetic anatomy and application in practice
Sanskrit pronunciation, traditional stotras, Kirtan, Ashtanga opening/closing mantras, Gayatri mantra
Supervised student teaching hours, peer feedback, observation, teaching evaluation and final assessment

faq.q: What level of yoga experience do I need for the 200hr TTC?
faq.a: We recommend at least 3–6 months of regular yoga practice before joining the 200hr course. You don't need to be advanced, but you should be comfortable with the basics. If you're a beginner, consider starting with our 100hr Foundation Course.

faq.q: Is Yoga Alliance registration included in the price?
faq.a: The course fee includes your school's Yoga Alliance graduation certificate and registration assistance. The Yoga Alliance annual membership fee ($120 USD) is paid directly to Yoga Alliance when you register online — this is standard industry practice.

faq.q: How many students are in each batch?
faq.a: We keep batches small — maximum 12–15 students — to ensure every student gets personalised attention and proper hands-on guidance from teachers.

faq.q: Can international students join from UK, USA, or Australia?
faq.a: Absolutely. Most of our 200hr students are international — from UK, USA, Australia, Canada, and Europe. Rishikesh is the world's yoga capital and we welcome students from all countries. You'll need an Indian e-Visa (easily obtained online) and fly into Delhi or Dehradun.

faq.q: Is the food vegetarian? Are there options for dietary requirements?
faq.a: All meals are sattvic vegetarian — no meat, fish, eggs, onion or garlic. We can accommodate vegan, gluten-free, and other dietary needs — just inform us at booking. The food is freshly cooked, nutritious and designed to support a rigorous yoga schedule.

faq.q: What is the cancellation policy?
faq.a: Cancellations 60+ days before start: full refund minus 10% processing fee. 30–60 days: 50% refund. Under 30 days: no refund, but you may transfer to the next available batch within 12 months.

programs.name: 300-Hour Advanced Yoga Training
programs.subtitle: Yoga Alliance RYT 500 Eligible
programs.certification: Yoga Alliance RYT 500 (combined with prior RYT 200)
programs.style: Advanced: Ashtanga Intermediate · Yin · Restorative · Yoga Therapy
included: 28 nights accommodation (shared room; private upgrade available)
3 yogic vegetarian meals daily + herbal teas
Yoga kit: mat, props, neti pot, essential oil kit

curriculum.detail: Ashtanga Second Series (Intermediate), advanced inversions, arm balances, backbends, pranayama asanas — going beyond the Primary Series
Full Yin Yoga training: meridian theory, target areas, props use, sequencing. Restorative yoga with props and therapeutic applications
Complete Yoga Nidra teacher training — multiple techniques, leading guided sessions, creating scripts, therapeutic use
Koshas (5 sheaths), Nadis (72,000 energy channels), Chakra system deep dive, Marma therapy points, Prana Vayus
Therapeutic applications for common conditions: back pain, anxiety, insomnia, hypertension, PCOD, knee injuries. Trauma-informed yoga teaching
Hatha Yoga Pradipika (all 4 chapters), selected Upanishads, Tantra philosophy, Vedanta, Samkhya-Yoga darshana
Kumbhaka (breath retention), Bandha mastery, advanced Kapalabhati, Khechari Mudra, full 12-technique pranayama system
Devanagari script, pronunciation refinement, mantra meaning, teaching in Sanskrit, Sanskrit etymology
Tri-dosha system (Vata, Pitta, Kapha), seasonal living, Ayurvedic diet, herbal tonics, Panchakarma basics
Theme-based class design, advanced cueing and assists, trauma-informed teaching, inclusive language, building a yoga business
Tibetan singing bowls, crystal bowls, tuning forks, nada yoga, structuring a sound bath session
Supervised teaching of multiple class styles, peer observation, video review, final teaching assessment

faq.q: Do I need my RYT 200 before joining the 300hr?
faq.a: Yes. The 300-hour course is an advanced training designed for practising yoga teachers. You must hold a Yoga Alliance RYT 200 certification (from any Yoga Alliance registered school) to enrol. Upon completion, you become eligible for RYT 500 — the highest standalone Yoga Alliance designation.

faq.q: Can I do the 200hr and 300hr back to back?
faq.a: Yes — many dedicated students complete both trainings in one trip to Rishikesh. We can schedule a short gap between batches for rest. You'd be in Rishikesh for approximately 53–55 days total.

faq.q: What is the difference between RYT 300 and RYT 500?
faq.a: RYT 300 is the credential for completing the 300-hour advanced training. RYT 500 is earned by combining a completed RYT 200 + RYT 300 from Yoga Alliance registered schools — it is the most advanced professional yoga teaching credential Yoga Alliance offers.

faq.q: Is Yoga Therapy taught in the 300hr?
faq.a: Yes. We cover foundational Yoga Therapy applications for common physical and mental health conditions including back pain, anxiety, insomnia, hypertension, PCOD and knee injuries. This is not a standalone yoga therapy certification but gives you practical therapeutic skills for your teaching.

title: Yoga Teacher Training in Bali
tagline: Island of the Gods · Tropical Villas · UNESCO Rice Terraces
description: Train in the world's most beautiful yoga destination — Bali. Practise in open-air shalahs with views of rice terraces and tropical jungle, live in a private villa, and immerse in Balinese Hindu spiritual culture. Bali attracts the most international yoga students globally, with a thriving community of teachers and seekers from every corner of the world.
setting: Ubud jungle villas & Canggu beach · tropical, 400m altitude
best_season: April – October (dry season); best months: May, June, September
airport: Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS), Denpasar — 1 hr to Ubud, 30 min to Canggu
highlights: Private villa accommodation with pool access
Multi-style: Hatha + Ashtanga + Vinyasa + Aerial + Yin
International cohort from UK, USA, Australia, Europe
Sound healing & acro yoga specialty workshops

programs.name: 100-Hour Yoga Immersion Bali
programs.subtitle: Foundation Course · School Certificate
programs.certification: School Certificate of Completion (pathway to 200hr RYT)
included: 12 nights villa room (shared twin or private upgrade)
Optional beach excursion (Dreamland or Echo Beach)
not_included: Indonesian visa (30-day visa-on-arrival free for many nationalities)

curriculum.detail: Hatha fundamentals, Ashtanga Sun Salutations A & B, standing and seated sequences, intro to inversions and arm balances
Breath-linked movement, creative sequencing, transitions and basic flow class structure
Yin postures and meridian theory — a Bali specialty addition to the standard curriculum
6 foundational breathing techniques with Bali-specific pranayama in nature settings
Morning seated practice, Yoga Nidra, walking meditation, Balinese sound healing introduction
Eight Limbs, Balinese Hindu spiritual traditions, chakra system, yoga history
Movement anatomy, safe alignment, common injuries and how to avoid them
Basic cues, class structure, peer observation and teaching practice

faq.q: Do I need a visa for Bali?
faq.a: Most nationalities (UK, USA, Australia, Canada, EU, India) receive a 30-day visa-on-arrival at Denpasar airport at no cost or minimal fee. You can extend to 60 days at the local immigration office. Always check the latest Indonesian visa rules for your passport.

faq.q: What is the accommodation like in Bali?
faq.a: Students stay in villa-style rooms — either shared twin or private (upgrade available). Villas feature lush garden settings, in some cases with pool access, and are located within walking distance of cafés, shops and the yoga shala.

faq.q: Is Bali safe for solo women travellers?
faq.a: Bali is one of the most solo-female-traveller-friendly destinations in Asia. The yoga community is welcoming, the local culture is respectful, and our training centres are in curated, safe neighbourhoods. Many of our students are solo women from Western countries.

faq.q: Why does the 100hr Bali cost more than Rishikesh?
faq.a: Bali's higher cost reflects villa accommodation, tropical destination experience, and the premium of the Bali yoga community. The 100hr Bali course adds Yin yoga, Balinese cultural ceremonies, and specialty workshops not included in Rishikesh, making it a distinctly different experience.

programs.name: 200-Hour Yoga Teacher Training Bali
programs.subtitle: Yoga Alliance RYT 200 Certification
programs.style: Multi-style: Hatha · Ashtanga · Vinyasa · Yin · Aerial (workshop)
included: 21 nights villa room (shared; private upgrade available)
Breakfast + lunch daily (2 meals) + herbal teas
Yoga kit: mat, blocks, strap, blanket
Cultural excursion (Ubud royal palace, rice terrace trek or Uluwatu temple)

curriculum.detail: 60+ classical asanas with alignment, muscle engagement, breath integration and therapeutic modifications
Full Primary Series led and Mysore-style practice, vinyasas, drishte, breath count — progressive over 21 days
Creative sequencing, peak pose methodology, transitions, theme-based classes, pacing and music integration
Complete Yin practice: 30+ yin postures, meridian theory, holding times, prop use and therapeutic sequencing
Introduction to aerial hammock yoga — beginner postures, inversions and restorative positions (2 workshop sessions)
10 classical techniques plus tropical breathing practices — morning pranayama in open-air shala setting
Multiple meditation styles: mantra, body scan, Vipassana, Yoga Nidra facilitation, sound healing immersion
Eight Limbs deep dive, Bhagavad Gita, chakra system, Balinese Hindu tradition and its parallels with yogic philosophy
Functional anatomy, skeletal and muscular systems, joint health, pregnancy yoga overview, injury management
Cueing styles, hands-on assists, sequencing theory, class design, ethical teaching, building a yoga career
Tibetan bowls, crystal bowls, nada yoga — structuring and leading a basic sound bath session
Supervised student teaching, peer and teacher feedback, observation of advanced classes, final teaching exam

daily_schedule.activity: Specialty workshop: Yin / Aerial / Acro / Sound

faq.q: What makes the Bali 200hr different from Rishikesh?
faq.a: The Bali program adds Yin Yoga, Aerial Yoga workshops, and Balinese cultural immersion. The setting is tropical villas vs ashram; the schedule is slightly less intense (dinner on your own gives you more flexibility). Bali attracts a highly international cohort — most students are from Australia, UK, USA and Europe.

faq.q: Can I work as a yoga teacher internationally with RYT 200?
faq.a: Yes. Yoga Alliance RYT 200 is the globally recognised standard for yoga teachers. With an RYT 200 from a Yoga Alliance registered school, you can teach at studios, gyms, retreats and wellness centres worldwide — in the UK, Australia, USA, Canada, Europe and beyond.

faq.q: What if I'm not flexible enough?
faq.a: You absolutely do not need to be flexible. Yoga teacher training is about learning, not performing. Many of our most skilled graduates started with limited flexibility. The training builds your practice progressively — physical flexibility will come naturally over the 21 days.

faq.q: Is there free time to explore Bali?
faq.a: Yes — Sundays are typically free or half-day. Evenings (from 6 PM onward) are free for dinner, exploring Ubud/Canggu, shopping or sightseeing. We also include a group cultural excursion to Balinese temples and rice terraces as part of the program.

programs.name: 300-Hour Advanced Yoga Training Bali
programs.subtitle: Yoga Alliance RYT 500 Eligible
programs.certification: Yoga Alliance RYT 500 (combined with prior RYT 200)
programs.style: Advanced: Yin · Restorative · Yoga Therapy · Somatic · Nidra
included: Cultural immersion: full-day Balinese ceremony experience

curriculum.detail: Advanced Ashtanga (Second Series introduction), handstands, forearm stands, scorpion, advanced backbends and drop-backs
Embodied anatomy, somatic awareness practices, fascia release, nervous system regulation through movement
Complete Yin teacher training — 50+ postures, full meridian theory (TCM), sequencing for specific therapeutic outcomes
Props-intensive restorative practice, nervous system restoration, working with anxiety and chronic stress
Full Yoga Nidra teacher training — iRest, classical Swami Satyananda, creating personalised scripts, therapeutic use
Kosha system, 72,000 Nadis, advanced chakra work, Marma therapy, Prana Vayus, Kundalini awareness
Therapeutic applications for: anxiety, depression, PTSD, back pain, hormonal imbalance, chronic fatigue. Trauma-informed teaching
Hatha Yoga Pradipika (complete text), Tantra Yoga philosophy, Kashmir Shaivism, non-dual teachings (Advaita Vedanta)
Professional sound healer training — Tibetan bowls, crystal bowls, gongs, voice toning, structuring a full healing session
Seasonal and circadian living (Dinacharya), Panchakarma overview, Ayurvedic cooking, herbs and self-care practices
Building a sustainable yoga teaching career: online classes, retreats, workshops, branding, social media, pricing, legal basics
Multiple class-style teaching assessments, case studies, supervised therapeutic sessions, final graduation assessment

faq.q: What is the prerequisite for the 300hr Bali program?
faq.a: You must hold a valid Yoga Alliance RYT 200 certification from any Yoga Alliance registered school. You should also have been actively teaching or practising for at least 6–12 months after your 200hr before joining the advanced training.

faq.q: Can I complete 200hr + 300hr in Bali on the same trip?
faq.a: Yes — we can arrange back-to-back batches. A combined 200hr + 300hr in Bali means approximately 49–51 days. Many serious students plan a 7–8 week Bali trip for the full RYT 500 journey.

title: Yoga Teacher Training in Goa
tagline: Arabian Sea · Beach Shalahs · Portuguese Heritage
description: Combine rigorous yoga teacher training with the laid-back magic of Goa — sun-soaked beaches, palm groves, vibrant café culture and the Arabian Sea. Goa sits perfectly between Rishikesh's spiritual depth and Bali's lifestyle appeal, offering a beachside yoga experience that's uniquely Indian yet internationally styled. Programs run October to May, perfectly timed for the best weather.
setting: North Goa (Arambol / Morjim) · beachside · sea level
best_season: October – March (peak); April – May (warm, less crowded)
airport: Goa International Airport (GOI), Dabolim / Mopa (Manohar) — 1.5 hrs to North Goa
highlights: Morning meditation on the Arabian Sea shore
Ayurvedic spa treatments at partner centres
Easy beach access, vibrant café and wellness culture
North Goa excursions: Arambol, Anjuna, Vagator

programs.name: 100-Hour Yoga Immersion Goa
programs.subtitle: Foundation Course · School Certificate
programs.certification: School Certificate of Completion (pathway to 200hr RYT)
included: 3 sattvic vegetarian meals daily + herbal teas
Yoga mat, blocks, strap & neti pot kit

curriculum.detail: Hatha foundational postures, Vinyasa flow basics, Yin yoga introduction — with beach practice sessions
6 essential breathing techniques with morning ocean-breeze pranayama practice on the beach
Sunrise beach meditation, mantra practice, Yoga Nidra, body scan relaxation
Eight Limbs, yoga history, Goan ayurvedic traditions, basic chakra system
Key joints and muscles used in yoga, safe alignment, common beginner injuries
How to cue, structure a basic class, beginner demonstration and observation practice
Neti, Nauli introduction, Kapalabhati — yogic cleansing for a clear, clean practice
Om chanting, Gayatri mantra, Ashtanga invocation mantras, basics of Sanskrit pronunciation

faq.q: When does Goa yoga training run?
faq.a: Goa programs run from October through May. The best months are November to February (perfect weather, 25–30°C). June to September is monsoon season and programs are paused.

faq.q: Can I go to the beach during training?
faq.a: Yes — afternoons (lunch to ~4 PM) and evenings after dinner are free. The beach is typically 5–15 minutes from the yoga school. Sundays may be fully free for exploration.

faq.q: Is Goa a good choice for international travellers?
faq.a: Absolutely. Goa is one of India's most international destinations, with excellent infrastructure, English widely spoken, and a relaxed atmosphere that's welcoming to first-time India visitors. It's a softer entry into India than Delhi or even Rishikesh.

included: 25 nights accommodation (shared room or private upgrade)
3 sattvic vegetarian meals daily + herbal teas

Creative and intelligent sequencing, transitions, peak pose design, multi-level class management
20+ Yin postures with meridian theory, long holding, prop use and therapeutic application — perfect for beach relaxation context
10 classical techniques including beach-environment breathing practices: Ujjayi, Nadi Shodhana, Bhramari, Kapalabhati, Bhastrika, Viloma and more
Sunrise beach meditation, mantra, body scan, Yoga Nidra, loving-kindness. Daily morning practice on the Arabian Sea shore
Patanjali Yoga Sutras, Bhagavad Gita key chapters, Sankhya darshana, Goan Ayurvedic traditions
Skeletal, muscular, nervous and respiratory systems applied to yoga. Functional anatomy, injury prevention and therapeutic basics

Supervised teaching, peer feedback, teacher evaluations, beach class teaching opportunity

faq.q: How is Goa's 200hr different from Rishikesh?
faq.a: Goa offers the same comprehensive Yoga Alliance RYT 200 curriculum as Rishikesh, with a beach lifestyle backdrop. Expect more free time, beach meditation sessions, and a laid-back atmosphere. Rishikesh is more ashram-disciplined; Goa balances discipline with enjoyment. Many students choose Goa if they want yoga training + Indian beach holiday in one trip.

faq.q: What is Goa's yoga scene like?
faq.a: Goa — especially North Goa (Arambol, Morjim) — has a thriving international yoga community. You'll find kirtan nights, cacao ceremonies, ecstatic dance, and yoga studios dotted between beach shacks and coconut groves. It's a genuinely yogic environment outside the school too.

faq.q: Can I combine a Goa yoga training with a Rishikesh or Bali training?
faq.a: Yes — many of our students complete 200hr in Rishikesh and then do the 300hr advanced training in Goa (or vice versa) on the same India trip. We can help you plan back-to-back trainings across multiple locations with a gap for travel.

daily_schedule.activity: Evening practice / Sound healing / Yoga Nidra

faq.q: Why choose Goa for the 300hr advanced training?
faq.a: Goa uniquely blends the deep Ayurvedic and yogic knowledge of India with a beachside environment that supports your wellbeing. The 300hr Goa program emphasises Ayurveda (more than Rishikesh or Bali) because Goa's coastal Ayurvedic tradition is exceptional. You also get genuine beach access between classes.

faq.q: Can I do 200hr in Rishikesh and 300hr in Goa?
faq.a: Absolutely, and many students do exactly this. You earn RYT 200 in Rishikesh (spiritual, ashram experience) and RYT 500 by completing 300hr in Goa (beach, Ayurveda focus). We can coordinate both bookings for you.
